item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This vs SCP 3002, let's go.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-1500 : did someone said… NO VEGETALS???

item.title: Ayyyy
item.content:encoded:
Attempts to discover the origin of SCP-1500's anomalous properties have thus far proven fruitless.

Ayyy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ OH MY GOD
- Ja

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …but he's not actually a plant. Have you read the second half?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: heh. fruitless. he's a plant. i get it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looked at this, figured it was a cheap self-insert, and downvoted.

Read the rest, got out the Imitrix, and upvoted.

item.title: That explains the headache I had...
item.content:encoded:
So, I was watching TV, and got this blinding headache. Went to bed, woke up with the same demn headache. Realized I'd been dreaming about my best bro, Zach. I don't have any brothers. Please help me…

Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A) You're right.
B) You're over a year late.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Greenish-grey? Sounds like Telekill alloy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a) pay attention to the dates on the messages you're replying to; that one was over two years old.

b) I should also point out that Schubert understood full well that the 'tree' is a coverup; that's the joke. Imagine that "Zachary" is a tree rewriting people's minds to be his friends because he's lonely, and to think he's a humanoid disguised as a tree because trees don't need friends. And then the joke falls apart because we've poked at it too much.

c) Not extradimensional, no.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not a tree, that was a coverup. It is actually a extradimensional metallic humanoid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really liked this one. The fake-out was well executed, and contrasted very nicely from the blah-ness of the first article. Big thanks to my good friend Zach, who recommended this to me. Thanks, bro!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OH MY GOD WHAT IS THAT THING AH AAGH AAGH HEAD HURTS…

*flops*
*awakens*

Hey Zack. Someone drugged my coffee, I think. Help me up.

"Sure thing, Dr. Stupid. Hey, you hear about that thing down in Samothrace?"

Ffff

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Reject is right, for starters. Most Keters are far from indestructible, but that's okay, because the Foundation doesn't try to destroy them, usually. Even if they did, though, this is actually more durable than you seem to think. This thing is as durable as a metal sculpture — it is one, as you'll find if you read Green-Gray.

It also has considerable telepathic powers that would make destroying it very hard to do practically. Anyone trying to do it themselves would be rapidly overcome and rendered unwilling to injure their old friend Zachary. You might be able to bypass this by using automated means, but there's no telling how the psychic juggernaut that is Zachary would react to being placed in any actual danger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's actually very outdated. We don't really destroy things anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Tiny tiny problem here.

Keter Policy

Such subjects must be cataloged, contained according to special containment procedures, and destroyed, if possible.

Seems like you could just burn this thing to be honest. Or cut it. Or saw it. Or just uproot it and dunk it in concrete or something. Make it a little bit more durable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I happen to be on an accidental binge of articles made by you, so- Good work!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this an old post, but-
That made me laugh. If I could upvote your comment, I would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, could you explain the unit conversion? Wolfram Alpha chokes and dies when I try "Rockwell Hardness into Gigapascals".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much, yeah. Also bits of the effect, though that's more tied into my headcanon than being terribly guessable. Honestly, the point wasn't to leave it as a puzzle. It was there mostly for myself.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All right, so. It comes down to the color (which, silly me, I always envisioned Telekill as greyish red because apparently I can't retain information at all) and the tensile strength, which is listed in gigapascals here and… I actually have no idea on the other page. Am I right? Is this the information we were all overlooking? I will definitely applaud you for hiding that well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Cripes, guys. It was an easter egg. A little throwaway reference (which, granted, involved checking annoyingly elaborate unit conversion tables) for myself and apparently the one guy who actually guessed it. Yes, I planned the article to be connected to another SCP. Didn't think mentioning that would get the response it did.

Anyhow, here's the origin story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great job, I really like this SCP. I read this at one point, and never bothered to upvote. I just spent the last hour and a half looking at every single article on the 1000 list for this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Heh, this is one of those times I forgot I upvoted already and tried to do so again. I think what this article does really well is creating this strange type of brooding menace you don't see very often, probably because it's very difficult to do well. I don't think there's another article on the site that makes the reader feel more like there's something he doesn't know which he really should, but at the same time makes him worried that he might not like what he finds out. In short, as far as suspense go, this is top notch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't care, post it anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
On my hands? From ripping out your lungs when you come to ask me to post the origin story?

I actually wrote the thing, but I'm not sure if it's actually any good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anaxagoras, you better get hustling on that origin story, or there's going to be blood on your hands.

(Too much?)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those definitions really need to be updated…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I believe this is one of those cases when destroying the SCP can be just as dangerous as keeping it- who knows what destroying it would do to the people it affected. We aren't even sure destroying its physical form will do anything. On the other hand, keeping it allows it to be studied, at least to some capacity, and if it can be studied its effects might be reversed or other positive developments may occur. I think that's a risk worth taking, all things considered.

(that aside, that page is a bit outdated, I think. The Foundation as it is written today very rarely attempts to destroy anything, even Keter SCPs)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Giant noob, but why don't they just kill it? Isn't that what they do with Keters they can neutralize? http://www.scp-wiki.net/keter

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, if that's really that excessive I might lower it. Or maybe not. I have some implausibility in here deliberately.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you failed to read the full article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Collected rainwater, laced with clays and nutrients, is to be pumped into the cell no fewer than two times per week.

We're trying to drown the skip slowly, I take it. More than likely it all gets pumped into the site's sewage system though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Same here.

And as has been said before, I think that you need to offer a bit more detail on the 'secret things' you're implying. Nobody seems to have got it yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A few hours after reading this SCP, something occurred to me:
As far as I can remember, I really do have a childhood friend named Zach.
I don't remember his last name.

Upvoted partly just for being a well-thought-out SCP, but partly because it's one of few SCPs that has hit that close to home for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know some people projectile vomit at the idea of Telekill, but the idea of this thing reaching out and influencing people so deeply is fucking terrifying, if we ever needed Telekill we need it for this mother fcker!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first, I had no idea why this thing's score was so high. I was this close to downvoting before I saw the collapsible.

Well done, you tricksy hobbit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I now imagine someone testing this on SCP-682 and having 682 trying desperately to free that nice Mr. Callahan it went on a fishing trip with back in the day…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh god… what if were all trees… and even we dont know it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The issue is that my mind may already be wiped, and my reaction in a containment breach had better be: "I shouldn't go near that cell! If I do, I might kill my poor Uncle Zach who's turning into a tree" rather than: "Gosh, it's dangerous in here. I better rescue my Uncle Zach before it's too late."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't understand why the Foundation would release misinformation to people who don't have high enough security clearance to know the truth. If I'm low-clearance, I get a report with [DATA EXPUNGED] slapped everywhere. Presumably, this report is released to people who are in some way involved with this SCP, probably its containment. If I'm some low-level chump who cleans the hallway next to this thing's containment cell and I read this report, when there's a containment breach, I'm not going to treat the SCP like a Keter-level threat and take immediate action (run like hell). I'm going to think it's Safe and continue dicking around while my mind is wiped.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're probably right, but hardness also doesn't feel quite right for something animate. Any thoughts?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think you would measure the strength of skin in tensile strength, that's more useful for building materials under compression or tension, you'd probably want some measure of hardness at a guess since skin is more to prevent piercing, tensile strength is more a property of bones or maybe flesh at a push.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, you've hinted at it a lot, and no one's figured it out, which has brought it to mythic proportions of aggravating "oh god just please tell us" at this point.

Oh god, just please tell us! D:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude! Cousins!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, you knew my uncle?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Actually, I think this SCP was inspired by the works of a certain Zach Callahan, old friend of mine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh, is it referencing something? I guess that would make sense for obscure hints.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Look, the thing I was hinting at is almost completely unimportant. And I wouldn't say I was constantly hinting about it. It's just something minor about this thing's origin. It's not particularly horrific or anything. It was just a mild curiosity I decided to work into this thing. I'd just like to hold onto it for now in case I actually finish figuring out how to make a decent story around that, since there might be something I can do.

You know what? Sure, I'll do it. I'll write some origin story for this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is very, very good.

My only problem, in fact, is that you constantly state you're hinting at something, while providing no clues as to what. No doubt there are reasons in the vein of wrecking the horror, but urgh I compulsively have to know.

Are there any plans to go further with the idea or is this really as far as it'll go? I'd love to see this hinted nature extend into a tale or something…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know nothing about that series beyond the fact that people never die there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Anax, I really have to ask… Were you inspired here by a certain popular manga/anime series?

+ Spoilers!
- Close me

Specifically, Shukuro Tsukishima from Bleach.

Also, I really like this. I of course especially like the misdirection of the first part.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ah alright, now I can definitely see the problem. Thanks Sorts!

I mean I liked the article before understanding but now I like it a lot more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Zach" is a monster that rewrites your memories and experiences of him to make you think that he's your best friend from the past, he makes you think that you love and adore him.

The implication is that he can and wants to use that influence to control people, and he's targeting military and political leaders. Think about the trouble you could cause if you could automatically insert yourself into the Old Boy's Network of any government, anywhere. And on the other hand, think about what you'd do if you found out the person you loved more than anything else in the world was locked up in some secret conspiracy lab and needed you to come rescue him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm pretty sure I'll be called stupid for this, but I don't see why it's dangerous. I read the whole article and the discussion thread multiple times, and I still can't see the danger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh god no

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i think i like the cover story better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now I'm picturing 682 under 1500's influence.

"Zach? Is that you? Why, we crawled out of the same spawning pool! How's it been, buddy? I haven't seen you in ages!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Foundation generally doesn't try to destroy SCPs, even poorly contained ones. Besides, it isn't doing too much yet, and what it's doing seems kind of self-preservation oriented, so trying to destroy it might be a bad idea. It's far from indestructible. It's tougher than any flesh, but no more so than a metal statue, which are of course perfectly possible to destroy. That said, it might be able to prevent its destruction through stopping the people, but the Foundation doesn't know what the limits of its capabilities are yet.

If you were to expose this to 682, it probably wouldn't do much. It's psychically very powerful by human standards, but 682 is probably a fair bit more resistant to psychic stuff than most humans, and 1500 is probably too durable for 682 to easily damage, assuming it even cares about 1500.
